bottom_line: Clarify test-impl agent's responsibility for pre-existing tests that assert old behavior being changed; investigate the HTML-cache spy test-isolation issue and fix it so it doesn't keep appearing as a blocked test.
coverage_notes: All step self-reports read in full (S01-S15). Fix-cycle prompts read in full. Workflow manifest read in full. Item-status JSON used for step inventory. No raw run logs available (worktree not present at .worktrees/I-00080/); analysis based on step self-reports + fix-cycle prompts. DB signal: yes (iw db-identity check confirmed UP throughout analysis).

findings.title: HTML-cache test blocked by test-isolation issue — implementation correct, test needs diagnostic
findings.recommendation: Investigate db_session.expire_all() + patch context interaction in test_i00080_html_view_caches_to_html_path_keyed_by_version. The cache-hit path in docs_html_view (line 142-144) is correct; the spy incorrectly records one render call on the second request. Try a fresh client instance or separate transaction context for the second request assertion.
evidence: ai-dev/active/I-00080/reports/I-00080_S07_Tests_report.md:48-53 — 'The caching logic works correctly — the html_path IS set... Yet the spy shows 1 call.'
ai-dev/active/I-00080/reports/I-00080_S09_CodeReviewFinal_report.md:104 — 'The implementation is correct; the test has an isolation or fixture interaction issue.'

ai-dev/active/I-00080/reports/I-00080_S09_CodeReviewFinal_report.md:112 — 'Investigate db_session.expire_all() + patch interaction. Try a fresh client instance or separate transaction for the second request assertion.'
findings.paste_prompt: /iw-new-incident Fix test-isolation issue in test_i00080_html_view_caches_to_html_path_keyed_by_version: spy shows render_markdown_with_callouts called on cache-hit second request when it shouldn't be. Implementation (docs.py:142-144) is correct. Likely db_session.expire_all() + patch context interaction. Analyzed in I-00080 self-assess report (see ai-dev/active/I-00080/reports/I-00080_self_assess_report.md). Target: tests/dashboard/test_i00080_docs_diagram_render.py. Effort: M.

findings.title: Pre-existing I-00074 test left failing across the full run; S07 test-impl didn't update it
findings.recommendation: Add to tests-impl prompt template: 'Also update any pre-existing tests in the repo that assert the old (pre-fix) behavior you are changing. Check tests/ for tests named test_i[0-9]+* that may cover the old behavior. Do not leave known-failing pre-existing tests as blockers.' This would have prevented S14 from triggering a fix cycle.
evidence: ai-dev/active/I-00080/reports/I-00080_S05_api-impl_report.md:75 — 'The 1 failure is pre-existing and intentional: test_i00074_docs_pdf_view_503_when_chromium_unavailable was written to assert the old (pre-fix) 503 behaviour. Our requirement 3 explicitly changes this.'
findings.paste_prompt: /iw-new-incident Add pre-existing-test update instruction to tests-impl prompt template; analyzed in I-00080 self-assess report (see ai-dev/active/I-00080/reports/I-00080_self_assess_report.md). Target: ai-dev/templates/Tests_Implementation_Template.md. Effort: S.

findings.title: Raw-DSL `<!-- purpose: ... -->` HTML comment strip location was ambiguous between design and implementation
findings.recommendation: In the Issue Design template, when an artifact (like a mapgen HTML comment) appears in the raw-DSL input stream, be explicit about which layer is the authoritative strip location. E.g., 'The <!-- purpose: ... --> HTML comment at the start of raw-DSL content must be stripped server-side in docs.py:_normalize_doc_content_for_render before markdown conversion.'
evidence: ai-dev/active/I-00080/reports/I-00080_S03_frontend-impl_report.md:36 — 'If S05 strips this comment server-side the regex simply finds nothing to strip — no harm done.'
ai-dev/active/I-00080/reports/I-00080_S05_api-impl_report.md:22 — 'S05 strips it server-side once here. S03's client shim's regex finds nothing to strip (no-op, safe).'
findings.paste_prompt: /iw-new-incident Clarify raw-DSL comment strip authority in Issue Design template; analyzed in I-00080 self-assess report (see ai-dev/active/I-00080/reports/I-00080_self_assess_report.md). Target: templates/design/Issue_Design_Template.md. Effort: S.
